rulings.Case Title: GARY ALLEN VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P) AS RELECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, October 6, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Essex Chemical Corporation¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. By failing to present contention interrogatory responses by plaintiffs, defendant did not satisfy its burden of production that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Allen was exposed to asbestos-containing materials or products attributable to defendant. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: HARRY ELLIOTT et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, October 6, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 5. Defendant Flowserve Corporation, successor-in-interest to Durametallic Corporation, incorrectly named as Durametallic Corporation¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is granted as to issues 3 (false representation), 4 (false representation/fraud/concert of action) and 6 (punitive damages) and denied as to issues 1 (negligence), 2 (strict liability) and 5 (loss of consortium). Plaintiffs presented sufficient evidence to create a triable issue whether Mr. Elliott was exposed to asbestos-containing packing attributable to defendant. The objections to Mr. Elliott¿s declaration are overruled. The court did not consider the evidence submitted with the reply papers. Plaintiffs concede the merit of issues 3, 4 and 6 as framed by defendant. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: HARRY ELLIOTT et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, October 6, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 5. Defendant FMC Corporation¿s motion for summary judgment on behalf of its former Peerless Pump business is treated as a motion for summary adjudication by defendant that it has no liability with regard to its former Peerless Pump business and, as so treated, the motion is granted. All of plaintiffs¿ procedural objections lack merit. Plaintiffs understood and were not prejudiced by the manner in which the motion was brought. The court exercises its discretion to look beyond the separate statement to consider plaintiffs¿ responses to defendant¿s contention interrogatories. Defendant sustained its burden of production and plaintiffs did not present any evidence creating a triable fact that Mr. Elliott exposed to any asbestos-containing product attributable to defendant¿s former Peerless Pump business. =(220/HEK)

All of plaintiffs¿ procedural objections lack merit. Plaintiffs understood and were not prejudiced by the manner in which the motion was brought. The court exercises its discretion to look beyond the separate statement to consider plaintiffs¿ responses to defendant¿s contention interrogatories. Defendant sustained its burden of production and plaintiffs did not present any evidence creating a triable fact that Mr. Elliott exposed to any asbestos-containing product attributable to defendant¿s former Chicago Pump business. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, October 6, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Defendant John K Bice Company, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment: to be heard in Dept. 206. Hearing required. =(220/JJM)

rulings.Case Title: LAURANCE HAGEN VS. ASSOCIATED INSULATION OF CALIFORNIA
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, October 6, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 3. Defendant Western Insulation, Inc.¿s demurrer is off calendar at the moving party¿s request. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: GEORGE MICHAEL SOTCAN et al VS. AC AND S, INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, October 6, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 4. Defendant Pfizer, Inc.¿s motion to transfer venue is denied. There is insufficient evidence that either the ends of justice or the convenience of the witnesses support transfer of this action as requested by defendant. =(220/HEK)
